# claude-bts

> Verify before you code — bts catches spec errors before they become debugging sessions.

bts is a CLI tool for Claude Code that structures the spec → verify → implement cycle. It plugs into Claude Code's lifecycle hooks to provide isolated verification, state tracking across sessions, and completion gates.

## Install

```bash
# Homebrew
brew tap imtemp-dev/tap && brew install bts

# Or one-line install
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/imtemp-dev/claude-bts/main/install.sh | bash

# Or from source (Go 1.22+)
git clone https://github.com/imtemp-dev/claude-bts.git && cd claude-bts && make install
```

## Initialize

```bash
cd your-project
bts init .
claude   # start Claude Code
```

This deploys skills, hooks, and rules to `.claude/` and creates `.bts/` for state management.

## Recipes

Recipes are structured workflows. Start one inside Claude Code:

```
/bts-recipe-blueprint add OAuth2 authentication
/bts-recipe-fix login bcrypt hash comparison fails
/bts-recipe-debug session drops after 5 minutes
/bts-recipe-design auth system architecture
/bts-recipe-analyze existing codebase
```

| Type | Purpose | Lifecycle |
|------|---------|-----------|
| blueprint | New feature (full spec → code) | discover → scope → research → wireframe → draft ←→ (assess/improve/verify/debate/simulate/audit) → finalize → implement ←→ test → simulate(code) → review → sync → complete |
| fix | Known bug | diagnose → fix-spec → implement ←→ test → complete |
| debug | Unknown bug | 6-perspective analysis → cross-reference → fix-spec → implement ←→ test → complete |
| design | Feature design (spec only) | research → draft ←→ verify → finalize |
| analyze | System understanding (spec only) | research → draft ←→ verify → finalize |

## Phases

Blueprint phases in order:
`discovery` → `scoping` → `research` → `wireframe` → `draft` → `assess` → `improve` → `verify` → `debate` → `simulate` → `audit` → `finalize` → `implement` → `test` → `review` → `sync` → `status` → `complete`

Implementation phases (shared across blueprint, fix, debug):
`implement` → `test` → `simulate`(code) → `review` → `sync` → `status` → `complete`

## File Structure

```
.bts/
├── config/              # git tracked — settings, template version
│   ├── settings.yaml
│   └── .template-version
├── specs/               # git tracked — recipe specs, project docs
│   ├── vision.md
│   ├── roadmap.md
│   ├── project-status.md
│   ├── project-map.md
│   ├── debates/{id}/        # project-level debates (meta.json + rounds)
│   └── recipes/{id}/
│       ├── recipe.json         # recipe state (id, type, topic, phase)
│       ├── intent.md           # discovery output
│       ├── scope.md            # scoping output
│       ├── wireframe.md        # structure design
│       ├── draft.md            # working spec
│       ├── final.md            # finalized spec (Level 3)
│       ├── manifest.json       # document relationships
│       ├── changelog.jsonl     # action log
│       ├── verify-log.jsonl    # verification history
│       ├── tasks.json          # implementation tasks
│       ├── test-results.json   # test results
│       ├── deviation.md        # spec-code differences
│       ├── review.md           # code review findings
│       ├── diagnosis.md        # fix/debug: root cause
│       ├── fix-spec.md         # fix/debug: change spec
│       ├── debates/{id}/       # recipe-level debates
│       └── simulations/        # simulation results
└── local/               # gitignored — runtime data
    ├── metrics.jsonl
    ├── work-state.json
    └── recipes/{id}/metrics.jsonl
```

## CLI Reference

```
bts init [dir]              Initialize project (--force to reinitialize)
bts doctor [recipe-id]      System and recipe health check (--strict)
bts validate [recipe-id]    JSON schema compliance check
bts verify <file>           Document consistency and level assessment (--no-code)
bts recipe status           Show active recipe
bts recipe list             List all recipes
bts recipe create           Create recipe (--type blueprint|fix|debug|design|analyze --topic "...")
bts recipe log <id>         Record action/phase/iteration (--phase, --action, --output, --force)
bts recipe cancel           Cancel active recipe
bts stats [recipe-id]       Metrics and cost estimation (--json, --csv)
bts graph [recipe-id]       Document relationship graph (--all)
bts sync-check [recipe-id]  Verify documents are in sync
bts debate list|log|resume|export   Manage debates
bts update                  Update templates to match binary version
bts version                 Show versions
```

## Skills (21)

Inside Claude Code, invoke skills with `/skill-name`:

| Skill | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| /bts-recipe-blueprint | Full spec-to-code lifecycle |
| /bts-recipe-design | Design spec (Level 2) |
| /bts-recipe-analyze | Analysis spec (Level 1) |
| /bts-recipe-fix | Bug fix with diagnosis |
| /bts-recipe-debug | Multi-perspective debugging |
| /bts-discover | Intent discovery conversation |
| /bts-wireframe | Structure design with mermaid diagrams |
| /bts-research | Codebase and external research |
| /bts-verify | Logical verification (fork context) |
| /bts-cross-check | Internal consistency check (fork context) |
| /bts-audit | Completeness audit (fork context) |
| /bts-assess | Level assessment and next-action decision |
| /bts-sync-check | Document synchronization check (fork context) |
| /bts-debate | Structured 3-expert debate |
| /bts-adjudicate | Evaluate debate conclusions |
| /bts-simulate | Scenario walkthrough — document or code (fork context) |
| /bts-implement | Code generation from finalized spec |
| /bts-test | Test generation and execution |
| /bts-review | Multi-perspective code review (fork context, 3 parallel agents) |
| /bts-sync | Sync spec with implementation |
| /bts-status | Project status aggregation |

## Models

Main session model handles all primary work and core quality gates.

Fork context agents run in separate AI instances (no shared conversation history).
Core gates use session model for maximum quality. Pattern-based checks use sonnet.

| Agent | Default | Setting |
|-------|---------|---------|
| verifier | session model | agents.verifier |
| auditor | session model | agents.auditor |
| simulator | session model | agents.simulator |
| reviewer_quality | session model | agents.reviewer_quality |
| reviewer_security | sonnet | agents.reviewer_security |
| reviewer_arch | session model | agents.reviewer_arch |
| reviewer_validator | sonnet | agents.reviewer_validator |
| reviewer_rebuttal | session model | agents.reviewer_rebuttal |
| simulator_validator | sonnet | agents.simulator_validator |
| simulator_rebuttal | session model | agents.simulator_rebuttal |
| diagnosis_validator | session model | agents.diagnosis_validator |
| diagnosis_rebuttal | session model | agents.diagnosis_rebuttal |

Override in `.bts/config/settings.yaml`. Options: sonnet, opus, haiku.

## Completion Gates

Specs complete when: verification.md exists, verify-log last entry shows critical=0 major=0. Output `<bts>DONE</bts>`. This sets phase to `finalize`.

Implementation completes when: all tasks done, tests pass, review.md exists, deviation.md exists. Output `<bts>IMPLEMENT DONE</bts>`.

Fix completes when: fix-spec.md exists, tests pass. Output `<bts>FIX DONE</bts>`.

## Key Rules

1. Every document modification triggers /bts-verify immediately after.
2. Verification runs in separate agent context (never self-review).
3. Edit draft.md in place (Edit tool), don't rewrite.
4. Log every action: `bts recipe log {id} --action [type] --output [path]`.
5. Run /bts-simulate at least once before Level 3.
6. Run /bts-adjudicate after every debate.
7. Run /bts-sync-check before finalizing.
8. Deviations are reports, not gates — they don't block completion.

## Document Levels

- Level 1 (Understanding): components listed, relationships explained, tech stack specified
- Level 2 (Design): data flow, error handling, interfaces, rationale for choices
- Level 3 (Implementation-ready): every file path, function signature, type, edge case, test scenario specified

---
bts version: 0.2.0
